revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Rokuzan''', also known as '''Roku-utaki''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on) : Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on) : Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on) : Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on) : Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on) : Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[20qq]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Completion Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 20qq-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on) : Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) : Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) : Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 20qq-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|20qq}} }}

== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) : Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) : Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 20qq-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|20qq}} }}
[[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[ja:六山]]
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{citation needed|むうやま}} [[Category:御嶽 (沖縄)]] [[Category:竹富町の歴史]] [[Category:竹富町の建築物]] [[Category:竹富島]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:A}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:A}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:六山]][[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mūyama]][[sn:ūyama]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ūyama]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mūyama}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mūyama}}

{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Rokuzan''', also known as '''Roku-utaki''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on) : Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on) : Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on) : Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on) : Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on) : Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[20qq]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Completion Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 20qq-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on) : Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) : Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) : Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|20qq|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 20qq-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|20qq}} }}

<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) : Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) : Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Qq" to "11"
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Rokuzan''', also known as '''Roku-utaki''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on) : Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on) : Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on) : Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on) : Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on) : Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Completion Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on) : Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) : Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) : Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}

Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}

{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Rokuzan''', also known as '''Roku-utaki''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on) : Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on) : Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on) : Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on) : Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on) : Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Completion Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on) : Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) : Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) : Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}

<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "11-1月4日 (旧暦)-11" to "11"
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Rokuzan''', also known as '''Roku-utaki''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on) : Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on) : Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on) : Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on) : Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on) : Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Completion Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on) : Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) : Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) : Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}
[[Category:New pages most likely ugoodbye $1 or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:六山]]
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{citation needed|むうやま}} [[Category:御嶽 (沖縄)]] [[Category:竹富町の歴史]] [[Category:竹富町の建築物]] [[Category:竹富島]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:A}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:A}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:六山]][[Category:New pages most likely ugoodbye $1 or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New pages most likely ugoodbye $1 or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New pages most likely ugoodbye $1 or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mūyama]][[sn:ūyama]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ūyama]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mūyama]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mūyama}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mūyama}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mūyama}}

{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Rokuzan''', also known as '''Roku-utaki''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. == Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Rokuzan''', also known as '''Roku-utaki''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on) : Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on) : Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on) : Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on) : Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on) : Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Completion tier 2]][[Category: Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on) : Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) : Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) : Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Rokuzan''', also known as '''Roku-utaki''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on) : Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on) : Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on) : Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on) : Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on) : Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Completion tier 2]][[Category: Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on) : Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) : Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) : Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}

{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on) : Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on) : Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on) : Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on) : Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on) : Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Completion tier 2]][[Category: Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on) : Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) : Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) : Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) : Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on) : Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.

Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.

**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽|12=draft}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|12=draft}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽|12=draft}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=draft}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{citation needed|むうやま}}
{{Okinawan Shrines}}

<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>

revision.comment: 28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:六山 up to rev 97901102
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|玻座間御嶽]] '''六山'''、'''六御嶽'''（ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}）は、[[沖縄県]][[八重山郡]][[竹富町]]の[[竹富島]]にある6つの[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]（竹富島では「ウタキ」ではなく「オン」と呼ぶ）である<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = 島立て伝説（六山） | publisher = 竹富島ゆがふ館 | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>。
== 概要 == 竹富島に28ヶ所ある御嶽の中で、『八重山島由来記』に記された6つの御嶽の総称である{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}。かつて竹富島には各地から渡来した6人の酋長がいて、守護神の招来を願ったところ6柱の神が招来されたのでそれぞれを御嶽に祀ったとされ、後には酋長も御嶽に祀られるようになったという<ref name="shima" />。これらの御嶽にはそれぞれ、神事を司る[[神司]]（かんつかさ）がいる。
== 六山一覧 == * '''玻座間御嶽'''（ウーリャオン） : 玻座間集落の北部に位置する。[[屋久島]]から渡来した根原カンドゥが拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。 * '''仲筋御嶽'''（サージオン） : 竹富小中学校の西に位置する。[[沖縄本島]]から渡来したアラシハナカサナリが拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。 * '''幸本御嶽'''（コントゥオン） : 仲筋集落の西部に位置する。[[久米島]]から渡来したコントゥフシカーラ（幸本節瓦）が拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。 * '''久間原御嶽'''（クマーラオン） : 東集落の東方に位置する。沖縄本島から渡来した久間原ハツガニが拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。 * '''花城御嶽'''（ハナックオン） : 久間原御嶽の先の小丘に位置する。沖縄本島から渡来したタカネドノ（他金殿）が拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。[[1957年]]及び[[2011年]]に改築されている<ref>{{Cite news | title = 花城御嶽で落成祝い 50年以上が経過し改築 | newspaper = 八重山毎日 | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>。 * '''波利若御嶽'''（バイヤオン） : 花城御嶽の向かいに位置する。[[徳之島]]から渡来したスーガードゥン（塩川トノ）が拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。 <gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|仲筋御嶽 File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|幸本御嶽 File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|久間原御嶽 File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|花城御嶽 File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|波利若御嶽 </gallery>
== 八山・九山 == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|国仲御嶽]] 六山に以下の2つの御嶽を加えたものを'''八山'''、'''八御嶽'''（ヤーヤマ）と呼ぶ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。 * '''国仲御嶽'''（フィナーオン） : 島のほぼ中央の国仲原に位置する。[[西塘 (琉球の人物)|西塘]]が[[首里]]の[[園比屋武御嶽]]の神を分祀したもの{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。 * '''清明御嶽'''（マイヌオン） : 竹富小中学校の西北に位置する。島を造ったシンミガナシとオモトテラスホーラスの2神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。結願祭での奉納芸能の舞台となる<ref>{{Cite news | title = 竹富島で結願祭 8点の芸能を奉納 | newspaper = 八重山毎日新聞 | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>。 さらに以下の御嶽を加えたものを'''九山'''、'''九御嶽'''（クヌヤマ）と呼ぶ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。 * '''西塘御嶽'''（ニシトウオン） : 玻座間集落のほぼ中央に位置する。西塘を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。 <gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|清明御嶽 File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|西塘御嶽 </gallery>
== 村御嶽 == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|世持御嶽]] 八山及び九山に加えられた3つの御嶽に世持御嶽を加えた4つの御嶽を'''村御嶽'''（ムラオン、トゥクルオン）と呼ぶ。村御嶽は、地縁団体法人竹富公民館が管理している{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}。 * '''国仲御嶽'''（フィナーオン） * '''清明御嶽'''（マイヌオン） * '''西塘御嶽'''（ニシトウオン） * '''世持御嶽'''（ユームチオン） : 玻座間御嶽の隣に位置する。火の神と世持ち神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。種子取祭での奉納芸能の舞台となる<ref>{{Cite news | title = 繁栄願い 芸能奉納 竹富島 | newspaper = 八重山毎日新聞 | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = 竹富町史 第二巻 竹富島 | editor = 竹富町史編集委員会 | date = 2011-10-31 |ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むうやま}} [[Category:御嶽 (沖縄)]] [[Category:竹富町の歴史]] [[Category:竹富町の建築物]] [[Category:竹富島]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[六山]] to [[Mūyama]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|玻座間御嶽]] '''六山'''、'''六御嶽'''（ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}）は、[[沖縄県]][[八重山郡]][[竹富町]]の[[竹富島]]にある6つの[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]（竹富島では「ウタキ」ではなく「オン」と呼ぶ）である<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = 島立て伝説（六山） | publisher = 竹富島ゆがふ館 | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>。
== 概要 == 竹富島に28ヶ所ある御嶽の中で、『八重山島由来記』に記された6つの御嶽の総称である{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}。かつて竹富島には各地から渡来した6人の酋長がいて、守護神の招来を願ったところ6柱の神が招来されたのでそれぞれを御嶽に祀ったとされ、後には酋長も御嶽に祀られるようになったという<ref name="shima" />。これらの御嶽にはそれぞれ、神事を司る[[神司]]（かんつかさ）がいる。
== 六山一覧 == * '''玻座間御嶽'''（ウーリャオン） : 玻座間集落の北部に位置する。[[屋久島]]から渡来した根原カンドゥが拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。 * '''仲筋御嶽'''（サージオン） : 竹富小中学校の西に位置する。[[沖縄本島]]から渡来したアラシハナカサナリが拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。 * '''幸本御嶽'''（コントゥオン） : 仲筋集落の西部に位置する。[[久米島]]から渡来したコントゥフシカーラ（幸本節瓦）が拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。 * '''久間原御嶽'''（クマーラオン） : 東集落の東方に位置する。沖縄本島から渡来した久間原ハツガニが拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。 * '''花城御嶽'''（ハナックオン） : 久間原御嶽の先の小丘に位置する。沖縄本島から渡来したタカネドノ（他金殿）が拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。[[1957年]]及び[[2011年]]に改築されている<ref>{{Cite news | title = 花城御嶽で落成祝い 50年以上が経過し改築 | newspaper = 八重山毎日 | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>。 * '''波利若御嶽'''（バイヤオン） : 花城御嶽の向かいに位置する。[[徳之島]]から渡来したスーガードゥン（塩川トノ）が拝み始めたとされる{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />。 <gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|仲筋御嶽 File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|幸本御嶽 File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|久間原御嶽 File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|花城御嶽 File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|波利若御嶽 </gallery>
== 八山・九山 == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|国仲御嶽]] 六山に以下の2つの御嶽を加えたものを'''八山'''、'''八御嶽'''（ヤーヤマ）と呼ぶ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。 * '''国仲御嶽'''（フィナーオン） : 島のほぼ中央の国仲原に位置する。[[西塘 (琉球の人物)|西塘]]が[[首里]]の[[園比屋武御嶽]]の神を分祀したもの{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。 * '''清明御嶽'''（マイヌオン） : 竹富小中学校の西北に位置する。島を造ったシンミガナシとオモトテラスホーラスの2神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。結願祭での奉納芸能の舞台となる<ref>{{Cite news | title = 竹富島で結願祭 8点の芸能を奉納 | newspaper = 八重山毎日新聞 | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>。 さらに以下の御嶽を加えたものを'''九山'''、'''九御嶽'''（クヌヤマ）と呼ぶ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。 * '''西塘御嶽'''（ニシトウオン） : 玻座間集落のほぼ中央に位置する。西塘を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。 <gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|清明御嶽 File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|西塘御嶽 </gallery>
== 村御嶽 == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|世持御嶽]] 八山及び九山に加えられた3つの御嶽に世持御嶽を加えた4つの御嶽を'''村御嶽'''（ムラオン、トゥクルオン）と呼ぶ。村御嶽は、地縁団体法人竹富公民館が管理している{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}。 * '''国仲御嶽'''（フィナーオン） * '''清明御嶽'''（マイヌオン） * '''西塘御嶽'''（ニシトウオン） * '''世持御嶽'''（ユームチオン） : 玻座間御嶽の隣に位置する。火の神と世持ち神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}。種子取祭での奉納芸能の舞台となる<ref>{{Cite news | title = 繁栄願い 芸能奉納 竹富島 | newspaper = 八重山毎日新聞 | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = 竹富町史 第二巻 竹富島 | editor = 竹富町史編集委員会 | date = 2011-10-31 |ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むうやま}} [[Category:御嶽 (沖縄)]] [[Category:竹富町の歴史]] [[Category:竹富町の建築物]] [[Category:竹富島]]
