revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{citation needed|むうやま}}
{{Okinawan Shrines}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:竹富町の歴史]] [[Category:竹富町の建築物]] [[Category:竹富島]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }}
[[sn:Mūyama]] [[ja:六山]] [[sn:ūyama]]

{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|ja|御嶽}} (sacred sites) on [[Taketomi Island]], [[Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)

Renovated in [[1957]] and again in [[2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|ja|園比屋武御嶽}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392326]] Wikidata name: Mūyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] (sacred sites) on [[:en:Taketomi Island|Taketomi Island]], [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[:en:Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantukasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[:en:Yakushima|Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[:en:Okinawa Island|Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[:en:Kume Island|Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[:en:1957|1957]] and again in [[:en:2011|2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[:en:Tokunoshima|Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of [[:en:Sonohyan-utaki|Sonohyan-utaki]], brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|qq=|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}}
{{Okinawan Shrines}}
{{translated page|ja|六山|version=97901102|comment=Imported full ja history}}

**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[:en:Yakushima|Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[:en:Okinawa Island|Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[:en:Kume Island|Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[:en:1957|1957]] and again in [[:en:2011|2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[:en:Tokunoshima|Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of [[:en:Sonohyan-utaki|Sonohyan-utaki]], brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392326]] Wikidata name: Mūyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] (sacred sites) on [[:en:Taketomi Island|Taketomi Island]], [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[:en:Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantukasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[:en:Yakushima|Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[:en:Okinawa Island|Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[:en:Kume Island|Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[:en:1957|1957]] and again in [[:en:2011|2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[:en:Tokunoshima|Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of [[:en:Sonohyan-utaki|Sonohyan-utaki]], brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of [[:en:Sonohyan-utaki|Sonohyan-utaki]], brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392326]] Wikidata name: Mūyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] (sacred sites) on [[:en:Taketomi Island|Taketomi Island]], [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[:en:Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantukasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[:en:Yakushima|Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[:en:Okinawa Island|Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[:en:Kume Island|Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[:en:1957|1957]] and again in [[:en:2011|2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[:en:Tokunoshima|Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>

**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of [[:en:Sonohyan-utaki|Sonohyan-utaki]], brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.

**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392326]] Wikidata name: Mūyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] (sacred sites) on [[:en:Taketomi Island|Taketomi Island]], [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[:en:Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantukasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[:en:Yakushima|Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[:en:Okinawa Island|Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[:en:Kume Island|Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[:en:1957|1957]] and again in [[:en:2011|2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[:en:Tokunoshima|Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of [[:en:Sonohyan-utaki|Sonohyan-utaki]], brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392326]] Wikidata name: Mūyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] (sacred sites) on [[:en:Taketomi Island|Taketomi Island]], [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[:en:Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantukasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[:en:Yakushima|Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[:en:Okinawa Island|Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[:en:Kume Island|Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[:en:1957|1957]] and again in [[:en:2011|2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[:en:Tokunoshima|Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of [[:en:Sonohyan-utaki|Sonohyan-utaki]], brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392326]] Wikidata name: Mūyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] (sacred sites) on [[:en:Taketomi Island|Taketomi Island]], [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi Town]], [[:en:Yaeyama District, Okinawa|Yaeyama District]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantukasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from [[:en:Yakushima|Yakushima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from [[:en:Okinawa Island|Okinawa Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from [[:en:Kume Island|Kume Island]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in [[:en:1957|1957]] and again in [[:en:2011|2011]]<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from [[:en:Tokunoshima|Tokunoshima]]{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of [[:en:Sonohyan-utaki|Sonohyan-utaki]], brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392326]] Wikidata name: Mūyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=Utaki}} (sacred sites) on {{ill|Taketomi Island|en|Taketomi Island|WD=Q474560|lt=Taketomi Island}}, {{ill|Taketomi, Okinawa|en|Taketomi, Okinawa|WD=Q699764|lt=Taketomi Town}}, {{ill|Yaeyama District, Okinawa|en|Yaeyama District, Okinawa|WD=Q629725|lt=Yaeyama District}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantukasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from {{ill|Yakushima|en|Yakushima|WD=Q242176|lt=Yakushima}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|WD=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Island}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from {{ill|Kume Island|en|Kume Island|WD=Q247515|lt=Kume Island}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957}} and again in {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from {{ill|Tokunoshima|en|Tokunoshima|WD=Q701847|lt=Tokunoshima}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|en|Sonohyan-utaki|WD=Q1053962|lt=Sonohyan-utaki}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}}
{{Okinawan Shrines}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]]
{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=Utaki}} (sacred sites) on {{ill|Taketomi Island|en|Taketomi Island|WD=Q474560|lt=Taketomi Island}}, {{ill|Taketomi, Okinawa|en|Taketomi, Okinawa|WD=Q699764|lt=Taketomi Town}}, {{ill|Yaeyama District, Okinawa|en|Yaeyama District, Okinawa|WD=Q629725|lt=Yaeyama District}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantukasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from {{ill|Yakushima|en|Yakushima|WD=Q242176|lt=Yakushima}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|WD=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Island}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from {{ill|Kume Island|en|Kume Island|WD=Q247515|lt=Kume Island}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957}} and again in {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from {{ill|Tokunoshima|en|Tokunoshima|WD=Q701847|lt=Tokunoshima}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|en|Sonohyan-utaki|WD=Q1053962|lt=Sonohyan-utaki}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}}
{{Okinawan Shrines}}
{{translated page|ja|六山|version=97901102|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六山]]
{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=Utaki|1=Ontake}} (sacred sites) on {{ill|Taketomi Island|en|Taketomi Island|WD=Q474560|lt=Taketomi Island|1=Taketomi Island}}, {{ill|Taketomi, Okinawa|en|Taketomi, Okinawa|WD=Q699764|lt=Taketomi Town|1=Taketomi, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Yaeyama District, Okinawa|en|Yaeyama District, Okinawa|WD=Q629725|lt=Yaeyama District|1=Yaeyama District, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantukasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from {{ill|Yakushima|en|Yakushima|WD=Q242176|lt=Yakushima|1=Yakushima}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|WD=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Island|1=Okinawa Island}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from {{ill|Kume Island|en|Kume Island|WD=Q247515|lt=Kume Island|1=Kume Island}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}} and again in {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}}<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from {{ill|Tokunoshima|en|Tokunoshima|WD=Q701847|lt=Tokunoshima|1=Tokunoshima}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|en|Sonohyan-utaki|WD=Q1053962|lt=Sonohyan-utaki|1=Sonohyan-utaki}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Q29413439|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 | title = Taketomi Town History Volume 2: Taketomi Island | editor = Taketomi Town History Editing Committee | date = 2011-10-31 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2011}} }}
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}}
{{Okinawan Shrines}}
{{translated page|ja|六山|version=97901102|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Mūyama |image=Yumuchi-On Taketomijima01s3s4592.jpg|thumb|right|caption=Hazama Utaki}} '''Mūyama, Mutounuyama 六山'''、'''六御嶽''' (ムーヤマ、ムトゥヌヤマ{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}), refers to six {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|qid=Q11071176|lt=Utaki|1=Ontake}} (sacred sites) on {{ill|Taketomi Island|en|Taketomi Island|qid=Q474560|lt=Taketomi Island|1=Taketomi Island}}, {{ill|Taketomi, Okinawa|en|Taketomi, Okinawa|qid=Q699764|lt=Taketomi Town|1=Taketomi, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Yaeyama District, Okinawa|en|Yaeyama District, Okinawa|qid=Q629725|lt=Yaeyama District|1=Yaeyama District, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}. On Taketomi Island, these sacred sites are called "On" instead of "Utaki"<ref name="shima">{{Cite web|和書| | url = http://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | title = Island Foundation Legend (Rokuzan) | publisher = Taketomi Island Yugafu-kan | accessdate = 2019-02-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210123170402/https://www.taketomijima.jp/museum/rekisi/shima.html | archivedate = 2021-01-23 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Among the 28 Utaki on Taketomi Island, the six mentioned in "Yaeyama Island Yuraiki" are collectively known as Rokuzan{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}. According to legend, six chiefs arrived on the island and, wishing to invite guardian deities, enshrined six deities at different locations. Over time, the chiefs themselves were also enshrined<ref name="shima" />. Each of these Utaki has a {{ill|Kantukasa|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantukasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or priestess, who presides over rituals.
== List of Rokuzan == * '''Hazama Utaki''' (Ūrya-on)
**Located north of Hazama settlement. Said to have been first worshipped by Nehara Kandū, who migrated from {{ill|Yakushima|en|Yakushima|qid=Q242176|lt=Yakushima|1=Yakushima}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Nakasuji Utaki''' (Sāji-on)
**Located west of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. First worshipped by Arashi Hanakasunari, who came from {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|qid=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Island|1=Okinawa Island}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kōmoto Utaki''' (Kontu-on)
**Located west of Nakasuji settlement. First worshipped by Kontu Fushikāra from {{ill|Kume Island|en|Kume Island|qid=Q247515|lt=Kume Island|1=Kume Island}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Kumahara Utaki''' (Kumāra-on)
**Located east of Higashi settlement. First worshipped by Kumahara Hatsugani, who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. * '''Hanashiro Utaki''' (Hanakku-on)
**Located on a small hill beyond Kumahara Utaki. First worshipped by Takane-dono (Takane-den), who came from Okinawa Island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />. Renovated in {{ill|1957|en|1957|qid=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}} and again in {{ill|2011|en|2011|qid=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}}<ref>{{Cite news | title = Hanashiro Utaki Complete Ceremony after 50+ Years | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | date = 2011-12-22 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>. * '''Hariwaka Utaki''' (Baiya-on)
**Located opposite Hanashiro Utaki. First worshipped by Sūgā-don (Shiokawa Tono) from {{ill|Tokunoshima|en|Tokunoshima|qid=Q701847|lt=Tokunoshima|1=Tokunoshima}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21}}<ref name="shima" />.
<gallery> File:Taketomi saji on.jpg|Nakasuji Utaki File:Taketomi kontou on.jpg|Kōmoto Utaki File:Taketomi kumara on.jpg|Kumahara Utaki File:Taketomi hanakku on.jpg|Hanashiro Utaki File:Taketomi baiya on.jpg|Hariwaka Utaki </gallery>
== Hachiyama and Kuyama == [[File:Taketomi fina on.jpg|thumb|right|Kunigami Utaki]] Adding the following two Utaki to the Rokuzan creates the '''Hachiyama''' (Eight Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on)
**Located in the central part of the island. Enshrines the deity of {{ill|Sonohyan-utaki|en|Sonohyan-utaki|qid=Q1053962|lt=Sonohyan-utaki|1=Sonohyan-utaki}}, brought by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Q29413439|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q29413439}}{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on)
**Located northwest of Taketomi Elementary and Junior High School. Enshrines Shinmi-ganashi and Omototerasu Hōrasu, the two gods who created the island{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Ketsugan Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Ketsugan Festival on Taketomi Island: Eight Performing Arts Dedicated | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/6024/ | date = 2006-04-10 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20120722210508/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/18987/ | archivedate = 2012-07-22 }}</ref>.
Adding the following Utaki to the Hachiyama creates the '''Kuyama''' (Nine Utaki){{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}: * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on)
**Located in the central part of Hazama settlement. Enshrines Sai Tō{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}.
<gallery> File:Taketomi mainu on.jpg|Seimei Utaki File:Nishito-On Taketomijima03s3s4500.jpg|Sai Tō Utaki </gallery>
== Village Utaki == [[File:Taketomi yumuchi on.jpg|thumb|right|Yumuchi Utaki]] Adding the following four Utaki to the Hachiyama and Kuyama creates the '''Village Utaki''' (Murautaki, Tokuru-on), managed by the Taketomi Public Hall{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=21-24}}: * '''Kunigami Utaki''' (Finā-on) * '''Seimei Utaki''' (Mainu-on) * '''Sai Tō Utaki''' (Nishitō-on) * '''Yumuchi Utaki''' (Yūmuchi-on)
**Located beside Hazama Utaki. Enshrines the Fire God and the World-holding God{{Sfn|町史|2011|p=24}}. Serves as the performance stage for the Tanedori Festival<ref>{{Cite news | title = Prosperity Prayers and Performances on Taketomi Island | newspaper = Yaeyama Mainichi Shimbun | url = http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | date = 2018-10-26 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20181026055559/http://www.y-mainichi.co.jp/news/34371/ | archivedate = 2018-10-26 }}</ref>.
